nodes.name: 南美洲
nodes.summary: 南美洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联70种鸟类，包括小美洲鸵、大美洲鸵、北山䳍等70种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联1443种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等1443种鸟类。

nodes.name: 反嘴鹬科
nodes.summary: 反嘴鹬科（Recurvirostridae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联6种鸟类，如斑长脚鹬、美洲反嘴鹬、Andean avocet等6种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鸻形目
nodes.summary: 鸻形目（Charadriiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联310种鸟类，如勺嘴鹬、短腿鸻、白鞘嘴鸥等310种鸟类。

nodes.name: 湖泊
nodes.summary: 湖泊是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联12种鸟类，包括疣鼻棲鴨、瘤鸭、美洲瘤鸭等12种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 湿地是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联30种鸟类，包括鵲鵝、澳洲硬尾鸭、黑天鹅等30种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

nodes.name: 污染
nodes.summary: 农药、工业污染与水体污染影响食物链安全。

nodes.name: 中美洲
nodes.summary: 中美洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联23种鸟类，包括大䳍、小穴䳍、灰胸穴䳍等23种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Black-necked stilt
nodes.summary: Black-necked stilt（Black-necked stilt，学名 Himantopus mexicanus），分类上属于鸻形目，并归入反嘴鹬科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联5个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括湿地，主要威胁包括污染。

nodes.name: 旧大陆
nodes.summary: 旧大陆是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联6种鸟类，包括大红鹳、Black-necked stilt、领燕鸻等6种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The black-necked stilt is found in estuarine, lacustrine, salt pond and emergent wetland habitats; it is generally a lowland bird but in Central America has been found up to ASL and commonly seen in llanos habitat in northern South America. It is also found in seasonally flooded wetlands

It is also found in seasonally flooded wetlands

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The black-necked stilt is found in estuarine, lacustrine, salt pond and emergent wetland habitats; it is generally a lowland bird but in Central America has been found up to ASL and commonly seen in llanos habitat in northern South America. It is also found in seasonally flooded wetlands

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Its genus is presently monotypic and seems to be closely related to the similar genus Allopyge, found in Old World cranes.Dronen et al

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: The black-necked stilt is found in estuarine, lacustrine, salt pond and emergent wetland habitats; it is generally a lowland bird but in Central America has been found up to ASL and commonly seen in llanos habitat in northern South America. It is also found in seasonally flooded wetlands

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Particularly the North American populations of the black-necked stilt have somewhat declined in the 20th century, mainly due to conversion of habitat for human use and pollution affecting both the birds directly as well as their food stocks
